title: qa_regression_resolved event payload
$comment: Provenance: authored 2026-04-23 in WU 3.4 as the QA regression resolution signal. Emitted by the qa-regression skill when a subsequent `qa_execution_completed` certifies the failing `test_id` passes at a commit SHA that post-dates the originally-filed regression (i.e., code has changed between the failing and resolving runs). Pairs with qa_regression_filed.schema.json; the idempotence key `(implementation_task_correlation_id, test_id)` is the same, and the `filed_event_ts` field makes the backlink explicit in the event log. If the regression had been previously escalated via the repeat-failure path (a `human_escalation` with reason `spinning_detected` on the original implementation task), the qa-regression skill additionally emits `escalation_resolved` (resolution_kind: qa_regression_resolved) in the same resolution pass — see escalation_resolved.schema.json.
description: Payload shape for the `qa_regression_resolved` event emitted by the QA agent on the resolution path. The envelope `correlation_id` is feature-level (matching the paired `qa_regression_filed`). The payload carries both identity fields of the resolved regression and the coordinates of the resolving qa-execution run, so a consumer can confirm — without replaying the full log — that a specific `(impl_task, test_id)` pair has been closed.

implementation_task_correlation_id.description: Task-level correlation ID of the ORIGINAL implementation task the resolved regression was attributed against. Matches the `implementation_task_correlation_id` field of the paired `qa_regression_filed` event exactly.

test_id.description: The kebab-case `test_id` whose re-execution passed and therefore resolves the open regression. Matches the `test_id` field of the paired `qa_regression_filed` event exactly.

filed_event_ts.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the paired `qa_regression_filed` event this resolution closes. Backlink into the feature event log; the `(implementation_task_correlation_id, test_id)` pair plus this timestamp uniquely identify the resolved regression.

resolving_qa_execution_event_ts.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the `qa_execution_completed` event whose all-pass outcome certifies this regression is closed. Backlink into the feature event log; a consumer can locate the passing execution evidence without re-scanning.

resolving_commit_sha.description: Git commit SHA (40-char full hex) against which the resolving qa-execution ran — i.e., the `commit_sha` field from the paired `qa_execution_completed` payload. Must differ from the paired `qa_regression_filed.failing_commit_sha` (a replay at the same commit is not a resolution — it is an idempotent skip).
